cards.back: A binary sequence describing the boundary of a shape, indicating the direction of the boundary at each pixel
A morphological operation that follows the boundary of a shape, moving pixels based on the chain code

cards.front: What is the purpose of Chain codes?
cards.back: To simplify the representation of a shape's boundary and to facilitate the implementation of morphological operations

cards.front: What is the difference between Chain code and Hit-and-run?
cards.back: Chain code describes the boundary at each pixel, whereas Hit-and-run uses a set of rules to determine the direction of movement

cards.front: What is the main use of Boundary following?
cards.back: To obtain a simplified representation of a shape's boundary, which can be used in further image processing operations
